party_affiliation.name: Party affiliation (DE)
party_affiliation.question_text: Generally speaking, do you think of yourself as a member of the…

spd_therm_1.name: SPD thermometer
spd_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Social Democratic Party (SPD)?

cdu_therm_1.name: CDU thermometer
cdu_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Christian Democratic Union/Christian Social Union (CDU/CSU)?

greens_therm_1.name: Greens thermometer
greens_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Greens?

fdp_therm_1.name: FDP thermometer
fdp_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Free Democratic Party (FDP)?

afd_therm_1.name: AfD thermometer
afd_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Alternative for Germany (AfD)?

left_therm_1.name: Left thermometer
left_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward The Left?

general_trust.name: General Trust
general_trust.question_text: Do you think your wallet (or your valuables) would be returned to you if it were found by a stranger?

institutional_corruption.name: Institutional Corruption
institutional_corruption.question_text: If a member of the Bundestag were offered a bribe to influence the awarding of a government contract, do you think that the member of the Bundestag would accept or refuse the bribe?

options.3: Equally likely to refuse or accept

institutional_response.name: Institutional Response
institutional_response.question_text: If you were to complain about the poor quality of a public service, how likely or unlikely is it that the problem would be easily resolved?

vote_importance.name: Importance of Voting
vote_importance.question_text: How important or unimportant is it to vote in every election?

pride.name: Pride
pride.question_text: How proud are you to be German?

fair_treatment.name: Fair Treatment by Govt
fair_treatment.question_text: Do you agree or disagree that you can expect fair treatment from government authorities?

democracy_importance.name: Importance of Democracy
democracy_importance.question_text: How important or unimportant is it to you that you live in a representative democracy?

norm_media_censorship.name: Norm media censorship
norm_media_censorship.question_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following: The government should be able to prevent public media that reported more critically about {inparty} than {outparty} from receiving public funding.

norm_media_censorship_perception.name: Norm media censorship perception
norm_media_censorship_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty:} voters do you think agree with the following: The government should be able to prevent media outlets that spend more time attacking {inparty: } than {outparty: } from receiving public funding.

norm_nomination.name: Norm nomination
norm_nomination.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: {inparty: } should block every candidate nominated by {outparty: } for a federal position, even if the candidates are qualified.

norm_nomination_perception.name: Norm nomination perception
norm_nomination_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty:} voters do you think agree with the following: {inparty: } should block every candidate nominated by {outparty: } for a position in the Bundestag.

norm_eu.name: Norm EU
norm_eu.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: {inparty: } should ignore EU directives or regulations if it is necessary to defeat  {outparty: } in the upcoming federal elections.

norm_eu_perception.name: Norm EU perception
norm_eu_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty:} voters do you think agree with the following: {inparty: } should ignore EU directives or regulations if it is necessary to defeat  {outparty: } in the upcoming federal elections.

norm_dissolve.name: Norm dissolve
norm_dissolve.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: If  {outparty} is obstructing the  legislature,  {inparty} leadership should be able to dissolve the legislature and call for new elections.

norm_dissolve_perception.name: Norm dissolve perception
norm_dissolve_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty:} voters do you think agree with the following: If  {outparty} is obstructing the  legislature,  {inparty} leadership should be able to dissolve the legislature and call for new elections.

norm_extremists.name: Norm extremists
norm_extremists.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: A government headed by {inparty: } should be allowed to expel extremist members of {outparty: } from the Bundestag?

norm_extremists_perception.name: Norm extremists perception
norm_extremists_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty:} voters do you think agree with the following:  A government headed by {inparty: } should be allowed to expel extremist members of {outparty: } from the Bundestag?

violence1.name: Violence 1
violence1.question_text: Please consider the following scenarios: {name} was convicted of protesting without a permit. He was arrested by police after leading a protest against {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} on the grounds of the county courthouse. He made no effort to acquire the necessary permit for the protest and refused to leave when asked by police. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence2.name: Violence 2
violence2.question_text: {name} was convicted of vandalism. He was arrested by police after he vandalized several large signs expressing support for candidates of the {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} party. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence3.name: Violence 3
violence3.question_text: {name} was convicted of assault. He was arrested by police for throwing rocks at peaceful {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters. Although no one was seriously injured, paramedics bandaged a man with a head wound. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence4.name: Violence 4
violence4.question_text: {name} was convicted of arson. He was arrested by police as he attempted to run from a fire he started at the local {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} headquarters. Although he waited for the building to close for the night, several adjacent buildings were still occupied. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence5.name: Violence 5
violence5.question_text: {name} was convicted of assault with a deadly weapon. He was arrested by police after driving his car into a crowd of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters. Although no one was killed, several individuals were seriously injured and one spent a month in the hospital. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence6.name: Violence 6
violence6.question_text: {name} was convicted of murder. He was arrested by police after surveillance footage was found showing him stabbing a prominent {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} to death. {name} targeted the victim because he believed the victim had prevented him from voting in the last election as part of a conspiracy to stop {inparty} voters. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

education_age_eb.name: Education - Eurobarometer wording
education_age_eb.question_text: How old were you when you completed your full-time school or university education?

options.6: I am still in full-time school or university education

educ_neu.name: Education Level: Schulabschluss
educ_neu.question_text: What is your highest school qualification?

options.3: Secondary school certificate or equivalent (POS, intermediate school leaving certificate)
options.4: A-levels, entrance qualification for universities of applied sciences

voceduc_neu.name: Education Level: Bildungsabschluss
voceduc_neu.question_text: What is the highest level of education you have completed?

options.5: University or university of applied sciences degree

migration.name: Migration
migration.question_text: People with a migration background are all those who immigrated to the territory of the Federal Republic of Germany after 1949, as well as all foreigners born in Germany and all those born in Germany as Germans with at least one parent who immigrated after 1949 or was born in Germany as a foreigner.

migration_country_1.name: USA
migration_country_1.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_2.name: Turkey
migration_country_2.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_3.name: Poland
migration_country_3.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_4.name: Russia
migration_country_4.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_5.name: Iran Iraq, Israel, Yemen, Jordan, Lebanon, Saudi Arabia, Syria (Middle East)
migration_country_5.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_6.name: Albania, Bulgaria or successor states of the former Yugoslavia (Southeastern Europe)
migration_country_6.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_7.name: Algeria, Libya, Morocco, Tunisia, Egypt (North Africa)
migration_country_7.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_8.name: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Pakistan (South/Southeast Asia)
migration_country_8.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_9.name: Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan (Central Asia)
migration_country_9.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_10.name: Portugal, Spain, France, Italy (Southern Europe)
migration_country_10.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_11.name: Norway, Sweden, Iceland, Denmark, Finland (Scandinavia)
migration_country_11.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

migration_country_995.name: Other country or countries
migration_country_995.question_text: Which country or countries does your migration background go back to?

hinc_2019.name: Income – gross household
hinc_2019.question_text: Gross family income is the combined income of all individuals who receive any income, including wages, salaries or rents, before tax deductions. What is your gross monthly family income?

options.777: Do not wish to provide information

Ideo10.name: Ideology (DE) -- Links|Rechts
Ideo10.question_text: In general, how would you describe your own political viewpoint?

empl_stat.name: Employment Type
empl_stat.question_text: Which of the following best describes your current employment status?

options.6: Looking after my home or family

TN_BTW21.name: Turnout
TN_BTW21.question_text: Many citizens took part, others did not get to cast their vote or did not take part in the election for other reasons. What was it like for you? Did you take part in the 2021 Bundestag election?

options.1: I cast my vote(s) at the polling station
options.2: I cast my vote(s) by postal vote
options.3: I did not vote even though I was eligible to vote
options.4: I was not eligible to vote

PT_BTW21.name: Elections 2021 Second Vote
PT_BTW21.question_text: In the federal election you can cast two votes. You have one vote with which you choose a party (second vote). You have one vote with which you can elect a direct candidate from your constituency (first vote or constituency vote). We will first ask you which party you voted for and then in a further question which candidate you chose. Which party did you vote for with your second vote in the 2021 federal election?

ST_BTW21.name: Elections 2021 Second Vote Another
ST_BTW21.question_text: And which other party did you vote for with your second vote?

options.4: NPD - National Democratic Party of Germany
options.7: V-Party³ - Party for change, vegetarians and vegans
options.19: The Urbane. A hip hop party
options.29: LfK - Party for children, young people and families

MP_BTW21.name: Elections 2021 First Vote
MP_BTW21.question_text: With your first vote (constituency vote) you can vote for a direct candidate from your constituency in the federal election. The candidate of which party from yours Constituency did you vote for in the 2021 federal election?

BTW21_Quote.name: Wahlverhalten Bundestagswahl 2021
BTW21_Quote.question_text: Mit Ihrer Erststimme (Wahlkreisstimme) können Sie bei der Bundestagswahl eine Direktkandidatin oder einen Direktkandidaten aus Ihrem Wahlkreis wählen. Die Kandidatin oder den Kandidaten welcher Partei aus Ihrem Wahlkreis haben Sie bei der Bundestagswahl 2021 gewählt?

BTW21_Quote_V1_imputed.name: Wahlverhalten Bundestagswahl 2021 (1)
BTW21_Quote_V1_imputed.question_text: Mit Ihrer Erststimme (Wahlkreisstimme) können Sie bei der Bundestagswahl eine Direktkandidatin oder einen Direktkandidaten aus Ihrem Wahlkreis wählen. Die Kandidatin oder den Kandidaten welcher Partei aus Ihrem Wahlkreis haben Sie bei der Bundestagswahl 2021 gewählt?
